sectionTitle: The Infrastructure of the AI Economy
sectionSubtitle: Eight capabilities that make HeadyEx the most transparent, precise, and powerful exchange for AI computational resources ever built.

features.title: API Credit Trading
features.description: Buy and sell Heady API credits on a transparent, metered exchange. Credits are the atomic unit of value in the HeadyEx marketplace — each credit represents a defined quantum of API compute, denominated in consistent usage units across all Heady services. Organizations with excess monthly allocation can list credits on the exchange; teams with spiky demand can source additional capacity at market rates rather than upgrading plan tiers.

features.title: Data Marketplace
features.description: HeadyEx hosts a curated marketplace for AI-ready datasets, pre-computed embeddings, vector stores, and knowledge base snapshots. Data sellers list assets with standardized metadata — source, freshness, domain, embedding model, vector dimensions, and license terms. Buyers can preview sample records, inspect quality metrics, and purchase access rights with granular freshness and update subscriptions.

features.title: Real-Time Metering
features.description: Every HeadyEx transaction is metered at microsecond resolution using OpenTelemetry-native instrumentation. Usage counters update in real time, credit balances are visible via WebSocket streaming, and budget alerts fire within 100ms of threshold breach. The metering engine processes 1M+ events per second with sub-millisecond attribution latency — no more end-of-month bill surprises.

features.title: Compute Arbitrage
features.description: HeadyEx enables compute arbitrage across multiple AI infrastructure providers — buying GPU burst capacity when provider spot prices are low and selling it as Heady compute credits at a margin. The arbitrage engine runs on φ-scaled pricing algorithms that balance supply/demand curves across GCP, AWS, Azure, CoreWeave, and Lambda Labs — passing savings to buyers while maintaining a liquid market.

features.title: Multi-Provider Settlement
features.description: HeadyEx settles transactions across multiple AI providers in a single unified billing interface. Whether a workload ran on Google Cloud Run, Cloudflare Workers AI, or a local HeadyOS node, all compute is denominated in Heady credits and settled monthly via a single invoice. Developers no longer need separate accounts with six different AI providers — HeadyEx handles the procurement, usage tracking, and settlement automatically.

features.title: Usage Analytics
features.description: A comprehensive analytics suite that surfaces credit consumption patterns, peak usage periods, cost efficiency metrics, and waste identification across all exchange markets. Attribution is granular to the individual agent, workflow step, or API call. Recommendations powered by Heady AI suggest optimization strategies — model downgrades for quality-insensitive tasks, off-peak scheduling, and caching opportunities — with projected savings quantified before implementation.

features.title: Smart Contracts
features.description: Planned for 2027: HeadyEx will support programmable exchange agreements — smart contracts that automatically execute credit trades, data subscriptions, and compute allocations when predefined conditions are met. Use cases include automated budget top-ups when credits fall below a threshold, conditional data subscriptions triggered by model performance thresholds, and revenue-sharing arrangements between publishers and distributors.

features.title: Cross-Ecosystem Exchange
features.description: HeadyEx is designed from the ground up as an open exchange — interoperable with credits and compute units from other AI platforms and cloud providers. Planned integrations include OpenAI credit conversion, Anthropic compute unit exchange, and Cloudflare Workers AI unit trading. The cross-ecosystem layer uses standardized AI Resource Units (ARUs) as a common denomination for cross-provider settlement.
